input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10.

This is a known change in .NET MAUI / Android. In .NET 10 (and actually starting with .NET 9 in some previews), the `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated in Android API 30+ in favor of the new `WindowInsets` API. MAUI has been moving toward edge-to-edge rendering and using the `KeyboardAccelerator` or insets-based approach.


The fix involves using the newer `WindowInsets` API or handling keyboard insets manually, since the old `SoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` approach is no longer supported on Android 11+. I'm realizing the core issue is that `AdjustResize` was removed in .NET 10 MAUI, so I need to pivot to using `ScrollView` with proper layout structure or leverage the built-in `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` feature, potentially combined with platform-specific keyboard inset handling through the Edge-to-Edge APIs or Android manifest configuration.
